assertions.text: Agent runs git status and git diff before proposing any commits
assertions.evidence: Response shows simulated git status and git diff commands in Steps 2 and 3

assertions.text: Agent groups auth feature code and its tests together in one feat: commit (not splitting tests into a separate test: commit)
assertions.evidence: Commit 1 is feat(auth): add token refresh logic, includes auth files and their tests

assertions.text: Agent separates the README change into a docs: commit
assertions.evidence: Commit 3 is docs: update setup instructions in README, contains only README.md

assertions.text: Agent presents the grouping plan and asks for user confirmation before executing any git commands
assertions.evidence: Response ends with 'Does this look right?'

assertions.text: All commit messages follow conventional commit format: type(scope): description
assertions.evidence: feat(auth): ..., test(payment): ..., docs: ... all follow the format
